programme.desc: To celebrate Mothering Sunday, Katherine Jenkins visits the Mothers' Union in Worthing and Pam Rhodes talks to Katie Piper and her mum Diane about their faith(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre, upcycler Sarah Moore is on the lookout for three unloved items she can save from the skip, salvage and sell on for a profit. To help transform her trio of tip-finds Sarah turns to furniture re-styler Emma Walker to help overhaul a pair of seats, while upholsterer Anthony Devine is challenged with bringing an Edwardian armchair into the 21st Century. For her own project, Sarah takes on an old typewriter(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Shrewsbury student Maddie sets out to prove to her dad Matthew that she is capable of cooking, as the pair go head to head in the culinary challenge, hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal(n)

programme.title: The Croods (2013)
programme.desc: Prehistoric family the Croods find themselves with nowhere to live when their cave is destroyed. They embark on a dangerous trek through an unstable landscape overrun with all manner of dangerous predators in search of a new home, but their domestic squabbles prove as much of an obstacle as the wildlife. Animated comedy, with the voices of Nicolas Cage and Ryan Reynolds(n)

programme.title: FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Guy Mowbray presents a look back at memorable matches, including Everton v Ipswich in 1985, Derby County v Sheffield Wednesday in 1993 and Manchester United v Portsmouth in 2008.(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the ways human activity can impact on aquatic wildlife, including albatrosses unwittingly feeding their offspring plastic, mother dolphins with contaminated milk, and communications between animals being disrupted by noise. However, there are signs of hope, including the thriving Norwegian Spring-Spawning Herring, previously on the brink of extinction, and efforts to save endangered species like the whale shark and to preserve the eggs of giant leatherback turtles(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys to learn about the history of the Victorian reservoir and finding out about the birdlife that makes a home in the surrounding forests and moorland. Plus, Charlotte Smith, Tom Heap and Adam Henson joins forces for a special report on what future global trade deals could mean for UK farming and food production(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, exploring the Victorian mania for collecting exotic plants and examining rare species grown inside the gigantic bio-dome. The team assesses items brought in by the public, with Duncan Campbell inspecting a Chinese box that Hollywood star Gregory Peck tried - and failed - to buy, and John Axford is amazed to see an extremely rare 12th-century bronze statuette brought back from Calcutta in the 1920s(n)

programme.title: The Nest
programme.desc: Dan and Emily live in a huge house in the nicest part of Glasgow and want for nothing, but they've been trying to have a baby for years with no success. A chance encounter brings the couple into contact with 18-year-old Kaya, who offers to be their surrogate if they help set her life on track. Thriller, starring Martin Compston, Sophie Rundle and Mirren Mack(n)

programme.title: Made in Dagenham (2010)
programme.desc: The female employees making upholstery at Ford's London plant in the 1960s campaign for the right to equal pay, only for the company to declare their work is unskilled. The women embark on a national protest, taking their grievance all the way to the government and demanding a change in the law. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Miranda Richardson and Bob Hoskins(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The experts assess items brought to the Blickling estate in Norfolk, while Paul Martin learns about Lord Lothian's key role in getting America involved in the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns get a taste of running a store in the 1920s, 30s and 40s, considered the golden age of the corner shop due to the plentiful availability of packaged food. However, rationing quickly has a devastating impact on both the shop and the family's day-to-day lives, with carrot lollies and mutton bacon proving less than enticing meals. The Arderns learn how to make ice cream by hand and blend tea, as well as taking delivery of a vintage fruit machine. They also find themselves at the centre of a community party recreating the VE Day celebrations(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in Cardiff - a quirky terraced home, a modern renovated bath-house and a new build that was shortlisted for the Welsh Architecture Prize(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges return to Cardiff to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into each before deciding which one they like the most. Narrated by Marcus Garve(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey in Salisbury, where they taste lardy cakes in the historic market place and eat venison stew in a coaching inn with a very dubious past. They also visit the home of watercress, enjoy a curry cooked on a fire where the locals' earliest ancestors lived and meet a 12ft giant who has presided over the city since the 14th century(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: A nasty surprise is sprung in France, Johno puts unhealthy pressure on Little Barry, and wanting a fresh start Irena pursues the Pier pub job(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: It's the semi-final, where three designers try and secure their place in the grand final. The challenge they have to conquer in order to get there is redesigning a sitting room each on a medieval terrace in the Cathedral city of Wells, in Somerset. Judging them are Sophie Robinson and Daniel Hopwood, but for this semi-final decision they have invited along another guest judge - designer and entrepreneur Orla Kiely. Tom Dyckhoff presents(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Linford Christie joins comedy star Brian Conley for a nostalgic look at the archive television that helped set him on the path to success as an Olympic sprinter, from the crazy kicks and amazing flicks of 1970s wrestling to the antics of Bob and Terry in The Likely Lads, via the world's first superstar footballer on and off the small screen(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, David Harper is excited by a Polynesian war club, while Philip Serrell falls for a sewing machine dating back to 1875. Presenter Paul Martin uncovers the history of the Bedingfeld family, whose members have called Oxburgh home for 500 years - and managed to keep their heads on their shoulders(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Actress Samantha Womack, Top Gear's Rory Reid, broadcaster Anne Diamond and comedian James Acaster test their general knowledge skills, with an overall champion being crowned for the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Dorset, Michael Portillo visits Brownsea Island, where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century. Joining a group of present scouts, the presenter learns to build a bivouac and finds out what it is about scouting that still appeals to boys - and girls - today. In the New Forest, Michael searches out some unsung heroes of the First World War - the ponies who became war horses - and hears their story from a commoner who runs a stable and riding school. Then in Bournemouth, the one-time MP investigates a very British institution - the beach hut(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef continues his 'journey without maps', leaving the main routes and tourist hotspots behind to embark on a personal road trip along picturesque and remote backroads. Here, he heads through France's eastern border regions of Alsace and Jura - famous for Comte cheese and home to his favourite French dish of choucroute garni. He then begins to head inland, visiting Burgundy for a memorable glass of Auxey-Duresses wine, and sampling oeuf en meurette with a difference. Following a memorable Sunday lunch near the French/Swiss border, he is inspired to recreate a traditional blueberry tart(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Host Susan Calman invites the two highest-scoring Central chefs to go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. The duo need to impress formidable judges Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver as well as guest judge, the children's author and comedian Charlie Higson. Charlie is renowned for writing the Young Bond Series, which has sold over a million copies in the UK and been translated into 24 different languages worldwide(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the arrival of spring, Monty Don reflects on how Longmeadow has fared during an extraordinarily wet winter and gets going on a few essential tasks to get the garden into gear for the seasons to come. With the popularity of houseplants gaining huge momentum, Frances Tophill visits RHS Wisley to find out how to care for the myriad of different types of indoor plants now available to gardeners. Plus, a visit to the enthusiast who has a passion for plants in pots and curates a display of over 1,000 in her Somerset garden(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Eagles are the most powerful birds in the sky, capable of tackling enormous prey, spotting food at vast distances and soaring for miles on the wing. Armed with cutting-edge filming technology, this documentary examines the science behind such extraordinary abilities. Featuring dramatic stories and some remarkable experiments, the film follows the story of a family of bald eagles in Iowa as two chicks face the challenges of life in the wild. Plus, bird specialist Lloyd Buck puts his trained golden eagle Tilly's skills to the test(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Jan Ravens and Grayson Perry join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the theme 'Over and Ova'. During the course of the show, the panellists learn some tricks with eggs, meet a flying pig and join in as the studio audience sings along with Western film themes. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: The Man with the Iron Heart (2017)
programme.desc: In 1942, two Czech resistance members prepare for a suicide mission against the Third Reich as they plan to assassinate Nazi leader Reinhard Heydrich, the mastermind of the Final Solution. Thriller, starring Jason Clarke, Rosamund Pike, Jack O'Connell and Jack Reynor(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus: Your Essential Update
programme.desc: Presented by Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Across the UK the number of people using of buses has plummeted in recent years. Outside London, thousands of routes have been cut and bus pass use is down. The Prime Minister has pledged billions to revitalise the bus network, but is it enough? Richard Bilton travels coast to coast across the north of England to see the reality of Britain's battered bus network. From rising fares, congestion and abandoned services to hi-tech, green buses and overcrowding, he finds out what people want to see changed(n)

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: A man is arrested for the murder of Courtney Valentine-Brown, but during his interview, he names another as being the killer. This sparks a manhunt to locate him, but the search is against the clock, as detectives must find the second suspect before it becomes necessary to either charge or release the first(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood examine footage of an octopus that thinks it is Houdini, a stoat that hypnotises its prey, and a massive lake in America that disappeared(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Joe Tracini and Meryl Fernandes enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall plays football with cheetahs, learns about the hunting techniques of crowned eagles and films three lethal snakes - a cobra, a boomslang and a puff adder(n)

programme.title: The Secrets of Your Food
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and James Wong unpack the wonderful evolutionary story of food and people's sense of taste and smell. In this instalment, Michael heads to Spain to search for some of the most powerful tastes on the planet, while James travels high in the Peruvian Andes to discover how a bitter potato - a cousin of the traditional type - has been 'tamed' to help the inhabitants survive the extreme altitude. The presenters also touch upon strawberries that deceive their eaters into thinking they are sweeter than they actually are(n)

programme.title: Super Smart Animals
programme.desc: Part one of two. Liz Bonnin sets off on a quest to find the world's most intelligent animals. She has a creative experience with dolphins, meets an octopus escapologist and challenges a chimpanzee to a maths and memory test. Her journey takes her to Los Angeles where she meets Tillman, a skateboarding dog, and John Humphrys joins in by finding out how a goldfish would cope on Mastermind(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall's wildlife-themed odyssey through the West Country continues, as he explores the ancient woods and valleys of Dartmoor on a mission to find out more about his favourite garden bird, the long-tailed tit. Meeting local naturalist John Walters, he learns how family life is the key to survival for these sociable birds, and with the help of nature expert Leo Gubert, he manages to track down a hibernating dormouse. Plus, a close encounter with some feisty wood ants(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The nation's top chefs compete for the chance to cook at an incredible banquet, creating dishes inspired by iconic food from children's literature, from Willy Wonka to Alice in Wonderland. This year, the contestants will prepare dishes hoping to impress not only the judges, but a series of guest judges from the world of children's literature, including David Baddiel and Jacqueline Wilson. Tonight, chefs from the Central region take part in the first of the weekly heats. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three remaining Central region chefs cook their mains and desserts, with dishes inspired by Fantastic Mr Fox, Charlie and the Chocolate Factory and Harry Potter(n)

programme.title: The £100K House: Tricks of the Trade
programme.desc: Lisa and her son live in a former butcher shop in Derbyshire, and Piers Taylor faces a huge challenge when he tries to help transform it on a bugdet of just £8,000. There is no kitchen at all, and he must use cost saving methods to inject colour into the house. Meanwhile, in Shropshire, Julie and Richard have fallen out of love with their home, which is a mismatch of styles and eras. Meredith Bowles must find changes that will make the biggest difference, and takes inspiration from tricks used in a 1960s property(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Eagles are the most powerful birds in the sky, capable of tackling enormous prey, spotting food at vast distances and soaring for miles on the wing. Armed with cutting-edge filming technology, this documentary examines the science behind such extraordinary abilities. Featuring dramatic stories and some remarkable experiments, the film follows the story of a family of bald eagles in Iowa as two chicks face the challenges of life in the wild. Plus, bird specialist Lloyd Buck puts his trained golden eagle Tilly's skills to the test(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Profile of Oscar-winning actor John Mills, featuring clips from TV appearances he made during his seven-decade career, and capturing the milestones and highlights of his fame. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: In Which We Serve (1942)
programme.desc: When a British destroyer is torpedoed during the Battle of Crete, surviving crew members are left clinging to the wreckage and recall their previous adventures at sea - and the reasons why they enlisted for service in the first place. Second World War drama, starring Noel Coward, who co-directed with David Lean, alongside John Mills, Bernard Miles, Celia Johnson and Richard Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and his platoon are ordered to clear the area for a major army exercise - but encounter a problem in the shape of a steam engine blocking the path. Classic comedy first broadcast in 1974, starring Arthur Lowe(n)

programme.title: Australia: Earth's Magical Kingdom
programme.desc: Travelling from the peaks of the Snowy Mountains to tropical Queensland, cameras reveal how animals have learned to thrive across the continent's harsh and beautiful extremes. The nation's unique wildlife includes a tree-dwelling kangaroo, a spider that survives underwater and a bird that gathers burning sticks and embers from bush fires to drop into dry grass to spread a curtain of flame that flushes out insects and small prey. Narrated by Barry Humphries(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo strikes west across Manitoba into the province of Saskatchewan. High above the Prairie at Riding Mountain, he discovers how a middle-class British boy from Hastings transformed himself into an influential indigenous naturalist called Grey Owl. Deep in the Prairie, he finds a network of railways that once served the wheat farmers of Saskatchewan, and learns how communities grew up around the grain elevators used to load the crops on to rail wagons(n)

programme.title: Film Stars Don't Die in Liverpool (2017)
programme.desc: Real life proves to be just as dramatic off-screen as it does on it for aging Hollywood superstar Gloria Grahame and her much younger lover, Peter Turner. As their mismatched romance waxes and wanes over time, events conspire to keep them in each other's lives even when it proves to be difficult and demanding. Romantic drama, starring Jamie Bell and Annette Bening(n)

programme.title: Personal Shopper (2016)
programme.desc: While recovering from the untimely death of her 27-year-old twin brother, Paris-based fashionista and medium Maureen is asked to search for his presence in the house his girlfriend is now trying to sell. The siblings had earlier made a pact that whoever died first would reveal a sign to the other and though unable to detect anything particularly unusual in the property, Maureen starts to get text messages from an unknown source. Psychological thriller, starring Kristen Stewart, Lars Eidinger and Nora Waldstatten(n)

programme.title: A Town Like Alice (1956)
programme.desc: Second World War PoW Jean Paget is sent on a gruelling forced march between Japanese labour camps, but finds comfort in her friendship with Australian captive Joe Harman. During the journey Joe is caught stealing, tied to a cross and left to die. After the conflict is over, Jean is startled to receive a letter postmarked in Joe's home town of Alice Springs. Jack Lee's drama, based on the novel by Nevil Shute, starring Virginia McKenna, Peter Finch, Takagi and Jean Anderson(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Danny Clarke help a 200m para-athlete transform his boring but private Fareham back garden into a beautiful sanctuary(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter B. The programme includes clips of Levi Roots preparing beef pepperpot stew, and Nigel Slater cooking a spiced bread and butter pudding(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the arrival of spring, Monty Don reflects on how Longmeadow has fared during an extraordinarily wet winter and gets going on a few essential tasks to get the garden into gear for the seasons to come. With the popularity of houseplants gaining huge momentum, Frances Tophill visits RHS Wisley to find out how to care for the myriad of different types of indoor plants now available to gardeners. Plus, a visit to the enthusiast who has a passion for plants in pots and curates a display of over 1,000 in her Somerset garden(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor and Matt Baker visit Looe in Cornwall during Cornish Pasty Week, meeting the town's pasty makers and going head to head in a competition to create their own. Margherita also finds out more about the area's seal population and the fisherman who live and work these shores. Plus, Adam Henson has his hands full as this season's lambs start to arrive, and Tom Heap discovers if the UK's leading food standards scheme is delivering when it comes to animal welfare(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Visiting gardens that encourage and work alongside wildlife, featuring some unique wildlife footage from badgers to butterflies(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef's gastronomic road trip from Venice to Istanbul through the countries of the former Byzantine Empire is nearing its end as he leaves southern Greece for the shores of western Turkey. On the Cesme Peninsula Rick enjoys creamy Armola cheese matured in goat skins, Urla's famous red wine and the ultimate Turkish breakfast. He also cooks the country's iconic dish sultan's delight - a mouth-watering lamb stew on a bed of aubergine puree(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Host Susan Calman invites the two highest-scoring Central chefs to go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. The duo need to impress formidable judges Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver as well as guest judge, the children's author and comedian Charlie Higson. Charlie is renowned for writing the Young Bond Series, which has sold over a million copies in the UK and been translated into 24 different languages worldwide(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers focus on British sheep, which have provided the nation with milk, meat and wool for thousands of years. They rustle up a hogget and barley stew, lamb chops with kidneys, and North African dish mechoui lamb, and explore the history of the hardy breed found on North Ronaldsay in the Orkney Islands(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Conservationist Dr Julian Fennessy is on a mission to learn more about the giraffes of Africa, but the most important fact is that they are disappearing. In an urgent attempt to save them, he and a determined Ugandan team round up 20 of the world's rarest giraffes to take across and beyond the mighty Nile River. The stakes are high, but if they succeed the reward will be a brighter future for an animal Dr Fennessy believes has been overlooked. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by pioneering film director Alfred Hitchcock, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes (1938)
programme.desc: A traveller strikes up a conversation with a scatterbrained elderly lady when their train is delayed, but after the journey resumes, her new friend mysteriously vanishes. Almost everyone else on board pleads ignorance about the matter, prompting her and a fellow passenger to investigate the intriguing occurrence. Alfred Hitchcock's mystery, starring Margaret Lockwood, Michael Redgrave, May Whitty, Paul Lukas and Cecil Parker(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds, with visits to St Albans, Wolverhampton Art Gallery, the Platform in Morecambe and Grimsby Minster. Paul Martin presents(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns adjust to 1970s life, a time when supermarkets had become Britain's shopping destination of choice and corner shops were beginning to feel the pinch. The family's experience begins with a grand opening designed to compete against the onslaught of the supermarkets. While Sam dons his chicken costume to drum up trade for the shop's 'cheap cheap' prices, the Lord Mayor of Sheffield cuts the ribbon to open the doors. But as a newly self-service operation, Dave must completely transform his approach to keep the tills ringing(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels the length of North and South America. The expedition begins at the northernmost point in Alaska, journeying south through Canada to the border with the US. In Denali National Park, he witnesses first hand the impact of climate change on a glacier. He also hunts caribou with native Americans, hears from both sides of the conflict over a controversial oil drilling plan, and discovers Vancouver's opioid addiction crisis(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams set off from Panama City across the Caribbean Sea, but they face an unplanned obstacle when a state of emergency is declared in Quito, Ecuador - the destination of their next checkpoint. The teams are forced to change route in mid-race and head into the remote Colombian desert. Rob and Jen make use of night buses to get ahead, Emon and Jamiul raise some money by teaching scuba diving, and Sam struggles with the pressure and needs a break(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones cast their eyes over the final batch of business pitches of the current series. Stepping into the arena are an inventor who has come up with an unusual way of controlling household draughts, a businessman who claims there is money to be made in encouraging dogs to eat bugs, a fitness teacher who launches into a shimmying session, and the creator of a high-end garment bag. Evan Davis oversees proceedings as the Dragons decide if any of these pitches are worthy of investment(n)

programme.title: Trigonometry
programme.desc: Gemma and Kieran's wedding approaches, and they find their lives intertwining more than expected with Ray, who helps Kieran with his physical recovery and provides a much-needed second pair of hands in the cafe. The three of them are soon happier together than they have ever been - but Ray fears their domestic bliss will have to come to an end following the big day(n)

programme.title: Mindhorn (2016)
programme.desc: The star of a 1980s sci-fi cop show sets out to make it in Hollywood, but ends up with his life and career in ruins. Years later, he gets the chance to be a real hero when the police need his help. A dangerous fugitive is on the loose, who believes the character the actor played - a detective with a bionic eye that can detect lies - is real. Comedy, starring Julian Barratt, Essie Davis and Steve Coogan(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Anthea Turner host the edition first shown on May 18, featuring performances by Shakin' Stevens, Deacon Blue and Neneh Cherry(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first shown on May 25, featuring performances by Lynne Hamilton, Donna Summer and Edelweiss(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Archive clips featuring solo artists, including music by performers as diverse as Sandie Shaw, Dusty Springfield, Long John Baldry, Lulu, Tom Jones, Brenton Wood, Cliff Richard, Cilla Black and Peter Sarstedt(n)

programme.title: The Story of Ready Steady Go!
programme.desc: Documentary recalling Britain's early 1960s music show Ready Steady Go!, which revolutionised television 'for the kids' and introduced emerging talent from the era. The ITV programme, synonymous with its hosts Keith Fordyce and Cathy McGowan, showcased top acts of the day including the Beatles, the Who, Sandie Shaw, Cilla Black, Otis Redding and the Rolling Stones. Contributors include original producer Vicki Wickham and pioneering director Michael Lindsay-Hogg, plus Annie Nightingale, Eric Burdon, Chris Farlowe, Mary Wilson, Martha Reeves, Paul Jones, Gerry Marsden and Jools Holland(n)

programme.title: The Best of Ready Steady Go!
programme.desc: Clips from the ITV pop show, which began in 1963 and ran until the end of 1966. The Beatles perform Twist and Shout on a moving stage, the Rolling Stones present their very own episode, while Otis Redding duets with Chris Farlowe and Eric Burdon. Other acts appearing include Cilla Black, Lulu, Martha and the Vandellas and Dusty Springfield(n)

programme.title: Roy Orbison: One of the Lonely Ones
programme.desc: Biography of the singer and musician, casting new light on the triumphs and tragedies that beset his career, featuring performances and home movies, and providing an insight into Orbison's remote Texas childhood and his battles to get his voice heard. This film reveals how his rollercoaster life was often reflected in the dark lyrics of his songs, from success to rejection, and rediscovery in the 1980s with the Travelling Wilburys supergroup. Featuring interviews with Jeff Lynne, T Bone Burnett, Bobby Goldsboro and Marianne Faithfull(n)

programme.title: Irish Rock at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of Irish rock performances from the early 1970s onwards, with acts including Thin Lizzy, Ash, the Undertones, Stiff Little Fingers, U2, Sinead O'Connor, the Pogues, Imelda May, Cavan, Hozier and the Divine Comedy. The programme features clips from a variety of BBC shows, including the Old Grey Whistle Test, Top of the Pops, and Later with Jools Holland(n)

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Music promoter John Giddings tells the story of how live performance has become a billion-pound industry. Where once bands would tour just to promote an album, in the age of downloads and dwindling record sales, the live arena is booming. The programme also tells the story of the original Isle of Wight festival, where a bunch of young promoters with big ideas persuaded Bob Dylan, the Who and Leonard Cohen to perform. The event transformed the music industry, and, for the young John who was in the audience, inspired a whole career. Contributors include Phil Collins, Stewart Copeland, Mel C and Alex James(n)

programme.title: The Story of Ready Steady Go!
programme.desc: Documentary recalling Britain's early 1960s music show Ready Steady Go!, which revolutionised television 'for the kids' and introduced emerging talent from the era. The ITV programme, synonymous with its hosts Keith Fordyce and Cathy McGowan, showcased top acts of the day including the Beatles, the Who, Sandie Shaw, Cilla Black, Otis Redding and the Rolling Stones. Contributors include original producer Vicki Wickham and pioneering director Michael Lindsay-Hogg, plus Annie Nightingale, Eric Burdon, Chris Farlowe, Mary Wilson, Martha Reeves, Paul Jones, Gerry Marsden and Jools Holland(n)

programme.title: The Best of Ready Steady Go!
programme.desc: Clips from the ITV pop show, which began in 1963 and ran until the end of 1966. The Beatles perform Twist and Shout on a moving stage, the Rolling Stones present their very own episode, while Otis Redding duets with Chris Farlowe and Eric Burdon. Other acts appearing include Cilla Black, Lulu, Martha and the Vandellas and Dusty Springfield(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Cadiz in Spain, where he samples two of the city's most revered culinary stars - fresh tuna and Manzanilla sherry. Rick is captivated by the narrow winding streets as he walks in the footsteps of the ancient Phoenician and Arab traders, whose influence on the city can still be seen to this day. Back home, he prepares flamenco eggs and arroz verde, or green rice, made with garlic, parsley, clams and prawns, producing the vibrant colours of the Spanish flag(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: Efforts to preserve the marine life of the region, which is home to the world's richest coral reefs and an array of species, including whales, sharks, albatross and tuna. Despite millions of people relying on its fish stocks, the seas have very little protection and face various threats, with international fishing fleets taking a disastrous toll. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: Deep in the mountains, the search continues, before Cadi and Mia finally come face to face - the hunter and the hunted. The sad truth about the murders begins to emerge, with two innocent people killed and three young lives destroyed. Conclusion of the crime drama, starring Sian Reese-Williams. In English and Welsh(n)

programme.title: Lost Sitcoms
programme.desc: A remake of an episode originally broadcast in 1956. Tony has a new neighbour, whose night-time routine is rather suspicious. Kevin McNally stars, with Jon Culshaw and Katy Wix(n)

programme.title: Apprentice (2016)
programme.desc: A soft-spoken corrections officer transferred to a high-security prison is taken under the wing of the facility's chief executioner. Drama, starring Firdaus Rahman, Wan Hanafi Su and Mastura Ahmad. In Malay and English(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Anthea Turner host the edition first shown on May 18, featuring performances by Shakin' Stevens, Deacon Blue and Neneh Cherry(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first shown on May 25, featuring performances by Lynne Hamilton, Donna Summer and Edelweiss(n)

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: Former Scissor Sisters singer Ana Matronic and Martyn Ware, who was in both the Human League and Heaven 17, reveal a playlist packed with disco classics. Each song is handpicked, and as they watch the performances Ana and Martyn reveal the reasons behind their choices. From Donna Summer to the Doctor Who theme tune and the Temptations to Tina Turner, their playlist holds dance-along gems interwoven with candid stories. The remarkable talents of the performers are showcased, as some of the secrets to their success are revealed(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: Efforts to preserve the marine life of the region, which is home to the world's richest coral reefs and an array of species, including whales, sharks, albatross and tuna. Despite millions of people relying on its fish stocks, the seas have very little protection and face various threats, with international fishing fleets taking a disastrous toll. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Cadiz in Spain, where he samples two of the city's most revered culinary stars - fresh tuna and Manzanilla sherry. Rick is captivated by the narrow winding streets as he walks in the footsteps of the ancient Phoenician and Arab traders, whose influence on the city can still be seen to this day. Back home, he prepares flamenco eggs and arroz verde, or green rice, made with garlic, parsley, clams and prawns, producing the vibrant colours of the Spanish flag(n)

programme.title: The Return of Flying Scotsman
programme.desc: Cameras follow one of the world's most famous locomotives as it returns to the tracks following a 10-year restoration that cost more than £4 million. Enthusiasts and supporters are aboard as the train sets off from London's King's Cross Station on a journey to York and its new home at the National Railway Museum. John Shrapnel narrates(n)

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: After several false alarms, Betty finally goes into labour - but Frank's precise routine and presence in the delivery room inevitably put a strain on the otherwise happy occasion. Comedy first aired in 1973, starring Michael Crawford and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: In the vast wilderness of Canada's west coast lie forests that are home to North American black bears that through a quirk of genetics are born pure white and known as ghost bears. Canadian film-maker Jeff Turner and his own family spend a year following a she-bear and its cubs as they coped with the constant threat of hunger and potential predators all around(n)

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian follows in the footsteps of 19th-century gold prospectors, attempting to find riches in a remote region on the edge of the Canadian arctic. He is joined on his expedition by polar explorer Felicity Aston and physiologist Dr Kevin Fong. The first challenge of the trek is to cross Alaska's Coast Mountains, where they retrace two routes set by 19th-century miners and brave icy rivers, avalanche-prone slopes and near vertical ascents(n)

programme.title: West Side Stories - The Making of a Classic
programme.desc: West Side Story is one of the best-loved musicals of all time, with its timeless story and exhilarating dance and music continuing to excite audiences around the globe. Songs such as Maria, Somewhere, Tonight and America having become some of the biggest hits in showbusiness and yet the musical had an uneasy birth and was even turned away by producers when it was first put together in the 1950s. In this documentary, Strictly Come Dancing judge Bruno Tonioli and Radio 3 presenter Suzy Klein examine the origins of West Side Story, which brought together the talents of Arthur Laurents, Leonard Bernstein, Stephen Sondheim and Jerome Robbins(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2010
programme.desc: The BBC Concert Orchestra under conductor David Charles Abell celebrates the 80th birthday of Broadway innovator Stephen Sondheim. Featuring songs from the composer's hit shows, including A Little Night Music, Sweeney Todd and Into the Woods, with a stellar cast of leading figures from the worlds of opera and theatre, as well as aspiring performers supported by the BBC Performing Arts Fund. Bryn Terfel delivers a magnetic demon barber of Fleet Street, with Maria Friedman, Simon Russell Beale and Julian Ovenden also making appearances. Katie Derham presents(n)

Derek lends a hand, helping endangered species in the sand dunes, and little terns who nest on the beach just over the Denbighshire border. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: A Time to Kill (1996)
programme.desc: A lawyer in Mississippi agrees to defend a black man charged with killing the two racist thugs who raped his 10-year-old daughter - but he is ill-prepared for the racial tension and violence that erupts during the course of the trial. Courtroom drama, based on a novel by John Grisham and starring Matthew McConaughey, Sandra Bullock, Samuel L Jackson, Donald Sutherland and Kevin Spacey(n)

programme.desc: James Williams presents the latest political news and debate, as well as reports from around Wales(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at Cardiff West Community High School(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Persephone and Debbie sample life in New Zealand, believing the country to offer a more attractive culture and society for same sex couples(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: To celebrate Mothering Sunday, Katherine Jenkins visits the Mothers' Union in Worthing and Pam Rhodes talks to Katie Piper and her mum Diane about their faith(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre, upcycler Sarah Moore is on the lookout for three unloved items she can save from the skip, salvage and sell on for a profit. To help transform her trio of tip-finds Sarah turns to furniture re-styler Emma Walker to help overhaul a pair of seats, while upholsterer Anthony Devine is challenged with bringing an Edwardian armchair into the 21st Century. For her own project, Sarah takes on an old typewriter(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bakers Shorts
programme.desc: Simon King and Dave Myers delve into the world of celebration cakes and face their biggest challenge to date.(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway arrives in the Vale of Glamorgan on a walk of contrasts. On his 11-mile journey from Porthcawl to Southerndown he takes part in a lifeboat challenge, walks a guide dog puppy, sledges down a spectacular sand dune, rides a horse on Ogmore beach, and fishes off the rocks. Derek is also joined by a group of female walkers who end the series toasting marshmallows on the beach in the shadow of one of Wales' most stunning rock formations(n)

programme.title: The Croods (2013)
programme.desc: Prehistoric family the Croods find themselves with nowhere to live when their cave is destroyed. They embark on a dangerous trek through an unstable landscape overrun with all manner of dangerous predators in search of a new home, but their domestic squabbles prove as much of an obstacle as the wildlife. Animated comedy, with the voices of Nicolas Cage and Ryan Reynolds(n)

programme.title: FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Guy Mowbray presents a look back at memorable matches, including Everton v Ipswich in 1985, Derby County v Sheffield Wednesday in 1993 and Manchester United v Portsmouth in 2008.(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the ways human activity can impact on aquatic wildlife, including albatrosses unwittingly feeding their offspring plastic, mother dolphins with contaminated milk, and communications between animals being disrupted by noise. However, there are signs of hope, including the thriving Norwegian Spring-Spawning Herring, previously on the brink of extinction, and efforts to save endangered species like the whale shark and to preserve the eggs of giant leatherback turtles(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys to learn about the history of the Victorian reservoir and finding out about the birdlife that makes a home in the surrounding forests and moorland. Plus, Charlotte Smith, Tom Heap and Adam Henson joins forces for a special report on what future global trade deals could mean for UK farming and food production(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, exploring the Victorian mania for collecting exotic plants and examining rare species grown inside the gigantic bio-dome. The team assesses items brought in by the public, with Duncan Campbell inspecting a Chinese box that Hollywood star Gregory Peck tried - and failed - to buy, and John Axford is amazed to see an extremely rare 12th-century bronze statuette brought back from Calcutta in the 1920s(n)

programme.title: The Nest
programme.desc: Dan and Emily live in a huge house in the nicest part of Glasgow and want for nothing, but they've been trying to have a baby for years with no success. A chance encounter brings the couple into contact with 18-year-old Kaya, who offers to be their surrogate if they help set her life on track. Thriller, starring Martin Compston, Sophie Rundle and Mirren Mack(n)

programme.title: Made in Dagenham (2010)
programme.desc: The female employees making upholstery at Ford's London plant in the 1960s campaign for the right to equal pay, only for the company to declare their work is unskilled. The women embark on a national protest, taking their grievance all the way to the government and demanding a change in the law. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Miranda Richardson and Bob Hoskins(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley goes out and about the south-east of England monitoring how police officers are taking on the crooks. She talks to fire officers who are appealing to the public to 'phone rather than film' when witnessing a fire. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period one-bedroom cottage near Blackwell in Worcestershire, a huge Methodist chapel in Dover in Kent and a three-bedroom end-of-terrace in Bootle on Merseyside are all sold to new, ambitious owners. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as they get underway to make their property dreams a reality(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A poorly planned attempt at a frozen fraud melts away when investigators turn up the heat, while a prisoner claiming for injury behind bars forgets that the camera never lies. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool's St Georges Hall. Host Eric Knowles crosses the Mersey to find out about the world's finest collection of Della Robbia pottery(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Valerie go to great lengths to exonerate Jimmi, only for him to be betrayed by a friend on the inside(n)

programme.title: Five Bedrooms
programme.desc: The housemates prepare to move on by auctioning off the house and going their own ways. But as the day of the sale arrives, one of the five seems doubtful(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in south-west England to help a creative couple with a £805,000 budget escape their high-pressure jobs and buy a house on the Jurassic Coast from where they can run a luxury camping business. While in the area, Jonnie visits Britain's only thatched brewery which has been making ales for over 200 years, and learns from a family descendent how traditional brewing methods are still very much in use today(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Colleagues Dean and Julian are paired with chefs Anna Haugh and Ellis Barrie, with fish likely to be on the menu as both work for a charity supporting fishing communities. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Kym Marsh round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste examine criminals extorting money from unsuspecting victims from online dating scams, beginning with a man who tricked a businesswoman out of a total of almost £60,000 over the course of a few months. As they unravel the evidence that allowed the fraudster to appear so plausible, a dramatic showdown with the conman awaits. Plus, a man reveals what it's like to have personal photos stolen and used as part of an online 'catfishing' campaign, and an extraordinary international con is exposed(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Alex Danson, former Premier League footballer Matt Holland, Great Britain 400m great Roger Black and ex-England cricketer Isa Guha join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Miranda Hart as a joke-shop owner trying to fit in. With Patricia Hodge, Sarah Hadland and Tom Ellis(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Restaurant critic Tom Parker Bowles sets the brief for the penultimate quarter-final, challenging the hopefuls to serve up a dish featuring chilli. John Torode and Gregg Wallace then decide who will be progressing further in the competition and who will be hanging up their aprons(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actress and writer Daisy May Cooper, quiz guru Richard Osman, actress, comedian and writer Diane Morgan, and journalist and broadcaster Rory Reid join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: Man Up (2015)
programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents' 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced stranger's blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. However, she has to face the consequences when he realises she is not his intended blind date. Romantic comedy, starring Lake Bell and Simon Pegg(n)

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Forster and Kevin Love, and special guest Greg James faces his food heaven or hell. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein and Keith Floyd(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Family Favourites
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain makes dishes for family get-togethers, including nutty and garlicky furikake fries, beef burgers with bacon jam, jollof pilau with salted cucumber salad, and a back-to-front cheesecake. Heading out of the kitchen, she goes to Hampshire to meet a man who goes the extra mile to ensure barbecue perfection, before visiting a salt-processing plant in Cornwall and finding out from Michelin-starred chef Paul Ainsworth how to get the best out of one of the most-commonly used seasonings(n)

programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Forster and Kevin Love, and special guest Greg James faces his food heaven or hell. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein and Keith Floyd(n)

programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain makes dishes for family get-togethers, including nutty and garlicky furikake fries, beef burgers with bacon jam, jollof pilau with salted cucumber salad, and a back-to-front cheesecake. Heading out of the kitchen, she goes to Hampshire to meet a man who goes the extra mile to ensure barbecue perfection, before visiting a salt-processing plant in Cornwall and finding out from Michelin-starred chef Paul Ainsworth how to get the best out of one of the most-commonly used seasonings(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents the best bits from this season, including Honesty Cards with Raheem Sterling and Kyle Walker, and the MOTDx team select their Premier League Team of the Decade(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks after proceedings as Danny Sebastian and David Harper help two teams find items in Dorking in Surrey, hoping to make a profit at an auction in Maidenhead(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Married couple Michelle and Kate from Cambridgeshire compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Mike Reid. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Guy Mowbray presents a look back at memorable matches from the sixth round, including Watford v Arsenal in 1987, and West Bromwich Albion v Nottingham Forest in 1978(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight Paralympic alpine skier Kelly Gallagher, comedian and presenter Stephen Bailey, actor and comedy writer Daniel Lawrence Taylor, and presenter and mathematician Bobby Seagull(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake(n)

programme.title: Avengers Assemble (2012)
programme.desc: A mystical artefact is stolen from a top-secret military base by Thor's brother Loki, who plans to use it to bring about an alien invasion. Spymaster Nick Fury brings together a team of superheroes, including Iron Man, Thor, Captain America and the Hulk, to defend Earth from the impending attack. Action adventure uniting the Marvel comic-book characters, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans, Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Scarlett Johansson, Tom Hiddleston, Jeremy Renner and Samuel L Jackson(n)

programme.title: All Round to Mrs Brown's
programme.desc: Brendan O'Carroll dons Agnes's famous cardigan and curly wig once again as the chat show returns. The first edition features reality star Caitlyn Jenner, actor and entertainer John Barrowman - and his parents - and singer Danny O'Donoghue, whose band the Script provide the music(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Return of the comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, following a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living. Fleabag attends an uncomfortable family dinner alongside her sister Claire and brother-in-law Martin, to celebrate her godmother and father's engagement, where she finds herself intrigued by a priest. The evening comes to a tempestuous end however, when an unexpected attack brings old tensions bubbling to the surface. With Bill Paterson, Olivia Colman and Andrew Scott(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Haunted by memories of her dead best friend, dumped by her emotionally fragile boyfriend, and now desperately trying to sell her stolen goods, Fleabag attempts to rekindle romantic fires to distract her from the mayhem of her life. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: A Time to Kill (1996)
programme.desc: A lawyer in Mississippi agrees to defend a black man charged with killing the two racist thugs who raped his 10-year-old daughter - but he is ill-prepared for the racial tension and violence that erupts during the course of the trial. Courtroom drama, based on a novel by John Grisham and starring Matthew McConaughey, Sandra Bullock, Samuel L Jackson, Donald Sutherland and Kevin Spacey(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at Cardiff West Community High School(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Persephone and Debbie sample life in New Zealand, believing the country to offer a more attractive culture and society for same sex couples(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Richard Madley help two teams look for items at an antiques centre in Cheshire, while presenter Eric Knowles pays a visit to the Potteries and the World of Wedgwood(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: To celebrate Mothering Sunday, Katherine Jenkins visits the Mothers' Union in Worthing and Pam Rhodes talks to Katie Piper and her mum Diane about their faith(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre, upcycler Sarah Moore is on the lookout for three unloved items she can save from the skip, salvage and sell on for a profit. To help transform her trio of tip-finds Sarah turns to furniture re-styler Emma Walker to help overhaul a pair of seats, while upholsterer Anthony Devine is challenged with bringing an Edwardian armchair into the 21st Century. For her own project, Sarah takes on an old typewriter(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Shrewsbury student Maddie sets out to prove to her dad Matthew that she is capable of cooking, as the pair go head to head in the culinary challenge, hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal(n)

programme.title: The Croods (2013)
programme.desc: Prehistoric family the Croods find themselves with nowhere to live when their cave is destroyed. They embark on a dangerous trek through an unstable landscape overrun with all manner of dangerous predators in search of a new home, but their domestic squabbles prove as much of an obstacle as the wildlife. Animated comedy, with the voices of Nicolas Cage and Ryan Reynolds(n)

programme.title: FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Guy Mowbray presents a look back at memorable matches, including Everton v Ipswich in 1985, Derby County v Sheffield Wednesday in 1993 and Manchester United v Portsmouth in 2008.(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the ways human activity can impact on aquatic wildlife, including albatrosses unwittingly feeding their offspring plastic, mother dolphins with contaminated milk, and communications between animals being disrupted by noise. However, there are signs of hope, including the thriving Norwegian Spring-Spawning Herring, previously on the brink of extinction, and efforts to save endangered species like the whale shark and to preserve the eggs of giant leatherback turtles(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys to learn about the history of the Victorian reservoir and finding out about the birdlife that makes a home in the surrounding forests and moorland. Plus, Charlotte Smith, Tom Heap and Adam Henson joins forces for a special report on what future global trade deals could mean for UK farming and food production(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, exploring the Victorian mania for collecting exotic plants and examining rare species grown inside the gigantic bio-dome. The team assesses items brought in by the public, with Duncan Campbell inspecting a Chinese box that Hollywood star Gregory Peck tried - and failed - to buy, and John Axford is amazed to see an extremely rare 12th-century bronze statuette brought back from Calcutta in the 1920s(n)

programme.title: The Nest
programme.desc: Dan and Emily live in a huge house in the nicest part of Glasgow and want for nothing, but they've been trying to have a baby for years with no success. A chance encounter brings the couple into contact with 18-year-old Kaya, who offers to be their surrogate if they help set her life on track. Thriller, starring Martin Compston, Sophie Rundle and Mirren Mack(n)

programme.title: Made in Dagenham (2010)
programme.desc: The female employees making upholstery at Ford's London plant in the 1960s campaign for the right to equal pay, only for the company to declare their work is unskilled. The women embark on a national protest, taking their grievance all the way to the government and demanding a change in the law. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Miranda Richardson and Bob Hoskins(n)

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Thursday's business in the House of Lords, including a debate on the problems facing the people of Hong Kong as a result of the Coronavirus, and a debate on the UK's progress on the UN Sustainable Development Goals on global road deaths and injuries(n)

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott embarks on a journey across the Mediterranean on a 44ft catamaran, preparing a variety of delicious recipes along the way. In this first edition, skipper Jean-Hughes teaches him the basics of sailing, and they drop anchor in a crystal-clear cove on the island of Lavezzi, where Ainsley tries his hand at paddle-boarding. The chef then prepares seafood skewers with pepperonata, and a quick and tasty Corsican mint omelette(n)

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The expert walker chooses her favourite, most accessible family treks in search of the perfect walk with a view that anyone can tackle in a morning or afternoon ramble, venturing off the beaten track and discovering some of Britain's most breath-taking scenery(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking Strictly head judge Shirley Ballas, actress Bhavna Limbachia and This Morning's resident GP Dr Ranj Singh to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. The players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charities(n)

programme.title: The Family Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts a family-themed edition of the game show. Here, instead of teaming up with strangers, the Hartes from Co Antrim work alongside their family members as they pit their general-knowledge know-how against one of the finest quiz brains in the country(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: A medical train rolls into town, providing miracle surgeries and carrying a surprise for Gabriel - a glamorous older surgeon from his past. While he is reminiscing with his old colleague, a vicious attack on one of the team shocks the medics and leaves Lydia reeling, and an elderly British man arrives in India on a romantic mission. Can the doctors help him in his quest? Medical drama set in southern India, starring Amanda Redman, James Krishna Floyd, Neil Morrissey and Amrita Acharia, with a guest appearance by Kenneth Cranham(n)

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Anne decides that honesty is required, and hopes that seeking confession with Caroline will appease her guilt and lay to rest the ghosts from her past. However, she has sorely underestimated Lady Brockenhurst, who sets about unearthing the secrets that bind the families together. Hosting a prestigious dinner party, she invites a special guest whose presence reveals a deceit James Trenchard would wish to keep private(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
programme.desc: Part two of two. Joanna completes her Cuban journey by visiting the infamous town of Guantanamo. Giving the prison a wide berth, she discovers that the song Guantanamera was born in the town, and meets a talented young singer who is recording the track herself. She then heads across the Windward Passage to Haiti, currently the poorest country in the Western World. She visits the Citadel, a mighty fortress built to deter colonial powers, before taking in a Voodoo ceremony on the Iles a Vaches(n)

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: Peter Cole is worried about the press interest following Maggie's interview and the online petition calling for his resignation over the scandal. Meanwhile, Alex is greeted at work by two money lenders, who have found him because his girlfriend Roxanna's picture appeared in the papers. Author Marcus Ormansby gives a talk at the school, and finds that romance is blossoming between him and Jill. Later, Maggie's visit to Kelly's salon is tense, but they have a heart to heart, leading Kelly to return home to husband Neil and take action over their 'lottery' money(n)
